name: Banana Cream Pie 3
recipeIngredient: Sugar substitute equivalent to 3 tablespoons sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare pie crust and chill for at least 2 hours before filling.
2. Soak gelatin in cold water and set aside.
3. Heat 1 1/2 cups of milk in the top of a double boiler over simmering water.
4. In a small bowl, combine flour, salt, and 1/2 cup of cold milk; mix until smooth and without lumps.
5. Slowly pour 1/2 cup of hot milk into the bowl with the flour mixture; stir to mix well.
6. Pour contents back into the pot of hot milk. Cook and stir over simmering water until thick and smooth, about 4 minutes.
7. Remove from heat. Add gelatin, sweetener, and vanilla; mix well to dissolve gelatin.
8. Cool and chill until mixture begins to set.
9. Peel bananas, slice thin, and measure 1 1/2 cups. Arrange 1 cup of sliced bananas in the bottom of the pie shell.
10. Carefully spoon and pour filling evenly over the bananas.
11. Arrange remaining 1/2 cup sliced bananas on top and garnish with vanilla crumbs saved during crust preparation.
12. Cover the whole pie carefully with plastic wrap and chill for 2-3 hours, until set and firm.
13. To serve, cut into eight equal pieces.
Note: Each serving contains 1 fruit exchange plus 1 low-fat milk exchange. You can use unsalted margarine to eliminate salt.
